_meta.author: scorecard-refine-local autopilot
_meta.note: Evidence-finalize Dallas TX city council batch (adam-bazaldua, bill-roth, cara-mendelsohn, chad-west, gay-donnell-willis). LOCAL tier, social-media + local-press + council-vote protocol. All five verified in office as of June 2026 (May 2025 election cycle; Roth via June 2025 runoff).

profile.background: Attorney and small-business owner; Army veteran (Bosnia/Hungary deployments); chairs council Finance Committee. First elected 2019; re-elected May 2025 (58.8%) — final term under Dallas's 4-term limit. Openly gay.

chad-west.notes_set: Evidence-finalized 2026-06-10 (local tier). Verified current: re-elected May 3, 2025 with 58.78% over Katrina Whatley; term-limited after 2025-27. Pro-choice on record (endorsed 2022 GRACE Act deprioritizing abortion enforcement; absent for the vote itself). Openly gay; Stonewall Democrats endorsement; Dallas Pride grand marshal. Deviation from progressive expectation: genuinely fiscally hawkish — chaired mayor-backed push to cut spending/taxes, backed FY2025-26 tax-rate cut; publicly anti-defund since 2020 ('it's not happening now') and shepherded budgets funding 350 recruits, though he opposed 2024 Dallas HERO Prop U (4,000-officer mandate) on fiscal grounds shared by the police union. Opposed DPD joining ICE 287(g) (Oct 2025). No findable positions on schools, elections, religious liberty, state-overreach cells, or gun policy — left null.

0.note: Publicly endorsed 2022 GRACE Act: constituents 'want to see the City of Dallas do everything in our power to protect the right to reproductive health access' (absent for vote but on record supporting).

1.note: Advocates protecting abortion access — documented contrary to abolition.

0.note: Openly gay; accepted Stonewall Democrats of Dallas endorsement ('always proud'); LGBTQ Victory Fund celebrated his win.

4.note: Dallas Pride parade grand marshal; promotes LGBTQ identity in public life as an official.

2.note: Finance chair leading mayor-backed spending/tax-cut push; backed FY2025-26 tax-rate cut (69.88 from 70.47); 'cut any kind of excess' on cost containment. Affirmative fiscal-restraint advocacy.

0.note: 2020: sponsored compromise keeping cut OT money in public safety, 'No one on council has tried to put an amendment forward to defund the police... and it's not happening now'; shepherded budgets funding 350 recruits. Note: opposed 2024 HERO Prop U staffing mandate on fiscal grounds (position shared by the Dallas Police Association).

2.note: Oct 2025 joint statement opposing DPD joining ICE 287(g) despite $25M federal offer — would turn police into 'an arm of federal immigration enforcement.'

profile.background: Former chef and Dallas ISD culinary-arts teacher; first elected 2019; re-elected May 2025 (57%) — final term under the 4-term limit. Former Mayor Pro Tem.

adam-bazaldua.notes_set: Evidence-finalized 2026-06-10 (local tier). Verified current: re-elected May 3, 2025 with 57%, avoiding a runoff; term-limited after 2025-27 (open D7 seat expected May 2027). Lead driver of the 2022 GRACE Act (deprioritizing abortion enforcement). Led 2022 committee resolution supporting the transgender community and condemning the Legislature; issued Transgender Day of Visibility proclamation; Stonewall Democrats endorsements. Authored the 2020 amendment cutting $7M from DPD overtime (passed 11-4 over the mayor's opposition); opposed 2024 Dallas HERO Props S/T/U ('political agendas,' warned of 'vigilantism'). Opposed ICE 287(g) partnership ('a betrayal of trust'). Demanded the State Fair ban firearms even for permit holders and criticized permitless carry. Moved to set a higher FY2024-25 property-tax-rate ceiling. Consistently progressive; no notable deviations found. Title note: Willis elected Deputy Mayor Pro Tem June 16, 2025 (KERA) — Bazaldua's pro-tem tenure ended.

0.note: Lead driver of the 2022 GRACE Act limiting abortion-related enforcement: 'we have much more important things to spend our resources on.'

1.note: Championed deprioritizing abortion enforcement — documented contrary to abolition.

0.note: Stonewall Democrats of Dallas and DGLA endorsements across cycles — hard contrary evidence on marriage cells.

2.note: Led 2022 committee resolution supporting the transgender community and condemning the Legislature ('No one should be condemned on identifying their gender by use of pronouns'); issued Transgender Day of Visibility proclamation.

4.note: Pushed updating the city anti-discrimination resolution to cover transgender/non-binary; active LGBTQ-policy promotion as an official.

2.note: Moved to set a HIGHER FY2024-25 tax-rate ceiling ('can always adopt a lower rate later'); backed FY2025-26 budget raising property-tax revenue $63.7M; warned against Prop U-driven cuts.

0.note: Authored 2020 Amendment 43 cutting $7M from DPD overtime (passed 11-4 over the mayor's opposition); opposed 2024 HERO police-staffing amendments as 'political agendas.'

Combative toward Austin (called Paxton's State Fair gun-ban suit 'political posturing'), but the local positions he defended (gun ban, anti-ICE) are scored on their own cells; no rubric-aligned overreach resistance found.

2.note: Opposed $25M DPD-ICE 287(g) partnership: officers would become 'an arm of federal immigration enforcement... a betrayal of trust.'

0.note: 'It's one thing to have a right to bear arms, it's another to have legislation, like permit-less carry, that makes it easier for senseless acts of gun violence'; demanded State Fair ban firearms even for permit holders.

profile.background: Attorney and commercial real-estate broker (GWU BA, SMU JD); 40+ year North Dallas resident; past president of Tiferet Israel Congregation and Jewish Family Service. Won the June 7, 2025 runoff 53.8% over Jeff Kitner, succeeding Jaynie Schultz; sworn in June 16, 2025.

bill-roth.notes_set: Evidence-finalized 2026-06-10 (local tier). Verified current: won June 7, 2025 D11 runoff 53.78%-46.22% over Jeff Kitner; sworn in June 16, 2025; term ends 2027. Declined to state a party ('the seat is, by design, nonpartisan'); endorsed by fiscal-conservative councilmember Cara Mendelsohn. Profile is fiscal/public-safety conservatism: campaign platform of 'holding the line and cutting taxes' and departmental audits; Aug 2025 memo proposing elimination of 14 programs (~$13.1M incl. the Office of Community Police Oversight, climate action planning) with savings to police/fire, infrastructure, and tax relief; flagged three programs for 'potential DEI non-compliance.' Spoke favorably of Prop U's police pay/staffing effects as a candidate. Social-issue record essentially blank — life, marriage, schools, elections, immigration, guns all null after search.

2.note: Platform: 'holding the line and cutting taxes,' 'cutting wasteful spending and conducting departmental audits,' performance-based budgeting; Aug 2025 memo proposing $13.1M in program eliminations driven by 'responsible stewardship of public funds.'

0.note: Public safety is his stated top priority (more patrol officers, lighting, community policing); spoke favorably of Prop U's pay/staffing effects toward the 4,000-officer minimum; proposed shifting oversight-office money to police/fire. Affirmative advocacy bar met.

profile.background: First elected 2019; re-elected May 2025 (~64%) — final term under the 4-term limit. Chairs Public Safety, Legislative Affairs, and Ad Hoc Ethics committees. Former Jewish Family Service of Dallas board chair; Texas Holocaust, Genocide, and Antisemitism Advisory Commission appointee.

cara-mendelsohn.notes_set: Evidence-finalized 2026-06-10 (local tier). Verified current: re-elected May 3, 2025 ~64%; term-limited after 2025-27; no announced run for other office found. The council's leading fiscal hawk: voted against the FY2024 budget AND tax rate (only she and the mayor), pushed deeper FY2026 cuts, urged voters to reject 2024 bond Props H and C favoring pay-as-you-go, sole no on the 2024 pension plan. Affirmative police advocate as Public Safety chair (moved hiring goal to 400/yr: 'this is a crisis'; sought $4.2M extra police/fire pay). Post-HERO: 'I agree with the sentiments' though she didn't support the propositions as written. No personal abortion stance found — her objections to the council's abortion-rights resolutions were procedural ('political theater... not appropriate for City Council'); absent for the GRACE Act vote. Deviation from R-lean: as Public Safety chair joined the near-unanimous Nov 2025 rejection of the $25M ICE 287(g) deal on staffing grounds ('We cannot afford not to have every officer on the street'). Marriage/LGBTQ, schools, elections, guns: null after search.

0.note: Called the council's abortion-rights resolution 'political theater at its worst,' but on procedural grounds (not a council matter); absent for the GRACE Act vote. No personal abortion position found — null.

2.note: Voted against FY2024 budget and tax rate seeking deeper cuts ('We are charged with taking money from people... this budget doesn't do that'); urged rejecting 2024 bond Props H/C, favoring pay-as-you-go and no new debt before a pension plan; sole no on the pension fix.

0.note: As Public Safety chair moved to raise the hiring goal to 400 officers/yr ('We simply do not have enough officers... this is a crisis'); sought $4.2M extra police/fire pay. Clear affirmative advocacy.

2.note: Joined near-unanimous committee rejection of the $25M DPD-ICE 287(g) partnership (Nov 2025). Stated grounds were staffing, not sanctuary ideology: 'We cannot afford not to have every officer on the street.'

set.office: City Council - District 13 (Deputy Mayor Pro Tem)

profile.background: Former President/CEO of Turtle Creek Conservancy; ad-agency and broadcast background; B.A. Texas Tech. First elected June 2021; re-elected May 2025 (65%). Elected Deputy Mayor Pro Tem June 16, 2025 (one-year appointment — verify after the ~June 2026 rotation).

gay-donnell-willis.notes_set: Evidence-finalized 2026-06-10 (local tier). Verified current: re-elected May 3, 2025 with 65% over Diane Benjamin; elected Deputy Mayor Pro Tem June 16, 2025 (Moreno is MPT; one-year appointments — title rotation due ~June 2026). Fiscal-conservative moderate: voted against the FY2024 budget/$120M tax increase (had proposed a lower rate), voted against expanding the 2024 bond to $1.25B, touts voting to lower taxes each year. Affirmative police-funding advocate ('If we can find more officers, we will find the money to pay them'; backed raising hiring goal 250 to 325; voted to fully fund DPD/DFR requests) but opposed the 2024 Dallas HERO Props S/T/U as written. Pride-friendly as an official: attended City Hall Pride flag-raising as DMPT, suggested painting library steps rainbow after the Oak Lawn crosswalk removal. Individual GRACE Act vote not recorded in press; no abortion statements found. Religion not publicly stated. Schools, elections, immigration, guns, state-overreach cells: null after search. Dallas Morning News endorsement 2025.

4.note: Attended City Hall Pride flag-raising as Deputy Mayor Pro Tem; proposed painting Oak Lawn Library steps rainbow after TxDOT forced crosswalk removal; called Dallas 'a destination city for the LGBTQ community.' Official promotion of LGBTQ identity.

2.note: Voted against FY2024 budget raising property-tax revenue ~$120M (proposed a lower 0.7358 rate); voted against the 9-6 increase of the 2024 bond package to $1.25B; backed cutting lobbyist funding to lower the FY2026 rate.

0.note: 'If we can find more officers, we will find the money to pay them' — supported raising the hiring goal 250 to 325; voted to fully fund DPD/DFR budget requests. Note: opposed 2024 HERO Props S/T/U as written (argued Prop S's immunity waiver would hurt recruitment).
